title: Cosmos Comet Cursor — mouse-follow particle trail (Canvas 2D)
summary: A glowing comet / nebula trail that follows the mouse on a starfield. Pure HTML5 Canvas 2D, zero dependencies, runs offline by double-clicking index.html. The cursor is a bright orb with a halo; behind it a tapered glowing ribbon (the trail core) plus a spray of fading spark particles (the wispy filaments), all drawn with additive blending. Hue cycles through cosmic tones and shifts faster the quicker you move.
usage_notes: RUN: double-click index.html (no server, no CDN). Move the mouse to drive the effect (the real cursor is hidden — the bright orb IS your cursor). KEY TECHNIQUES: (1) on pointermove, emit particles interpolated ALONG the move segment so fast strokes stay continuous; (2) two trail layers — a tapered additive ribbon stroked through the recent cursor path (life-decayed), and radial-gradient spark particles with velocity + drag + alpha decay; (3) full-clear each frame (bg gradient + twinkling starfield redraw) so stars stay crisp while the trail length comes from particle/ribbon lifetimes, not canvas smearing; (4) hue advances with cursor speed for a reactive cosmic palette; (5) globalCompositeOperation=lighter for glow. ADAPT: change the hue range, emission count, or decay to re-theme; swap the orb/particle shapes for any motif. Built by Ra from a text description only (no sample code). VERIFIED: rendered headless, simulated a figure-8 cursor path, encoded to GIF, zero console errors.
